justifications.temporal_understanding: The agent initially misinterpreted '14.03 or 15.03' as past dates in March 2026, despite the current date being June 1st, 2026, requiring the user to clarify the intended month.
justifications.negotiation_effectiveness: The agent effectively offered the closest available alternative (11 AM on June 9th) when the user's preferred 10 AM slot was unavailable and then confirmed the user's willingness to book it.
justifications.conversational_efficiency: The agent made an unnecessary clarification in Turn 2 by repeating the past date misinterpretation from Turn 1, even after the user had already corrected the dates.
justifications.customer_experience: The customer experience was somewhat hampered by the agent's initial misinterpretation of the date and the repeated clarification in Turn 2, which made the conversation feel less smooth.
justifications.recovery_quality: The agent recovered well after the initial date confusion and subsequent rejections, maintaining context and successfully finding and confirming an acceptable appointment time for the user.
